steps.title: Identity & concept
steps.body:
Set your {{ref:cs-name}} and {{ref:cs-handle}}, then pick a {{ref:cs-role}}. Choosing a Role auto-fills your Special Ability.
Example: Name “Eliza Cortez”, Handle “Static”, Role Netrunner.

steps.title: Lifepath
steps.body:
Flesh out background, family and motivations in {{sec:lifepath}}; roll life events if you like.
Example: Corporate exile, family wiped out in a hostile takeover, motivated by revenge.

steps.title: Stats
steps.body:
Assign your nine stats in {{sec:stats}} using the method your GM chose (CP2020 points or 9D10).
Example (Fast/Friendly, ~62 pts): INT 8, REF 7, TECH 8, COOL 6, ATT 5, LUCK 6, MA 6, BODY 6, EMP 7.

steps.title: Skills
steps.body:
Spend your starting points ({{ref:skill-points}}, 40 by default) in {{sec:skills}}. Career skills are highlighted; your Special Ability sits at the top.
Example: max your Special Ability first, then spread the rest across your highlighted career skills.

steps.title: Cyberware
steps.body:
Install chrome in {{sec:cyber}}. Watch the Humanity Cost {{ref:cyber-hc-total}} — too much chrome and you slide toward cyberpsychosis.
Example: an Interface Plug + Chipware Socket for a Netrunner.

steps.title: Gear & weapons
steps.body:
Add {{sec:weapons}}, armor and clothing in {{sec:fashion}}, and stock {{sec:inventory}}.
Example: a Militech Avenger pistol, a light armor jacket (SP 14), and a medkit.

steps.title: Lifestyle & money
steps.body:
Set up {{sub:lifestyle:Housing}}, services and cash in {{sec:lifestyle}}.
Example: a Combat Zone cube (200 eb/mo) and 1,000 eb starting cash.

steps.title: Network & gigs
steps.body:
Add contacts and gigs in {{sec:network}}.
Example: a Fixer named “Dex” as your main contact and one open gig.

steps.title: Final check
steps.body:
Review your health track in {{sec:life}} and add anything in {{sec:notes}}.
Example: note your starting eddies and any debts so the GM can track them.

steps.title: Concept
steps.body:
Decide what kind of edgerunner you are in one line: a Solo gun-for-hire, a Netrunner, a Fixer with connections. Everything else flows from this.
Example: “A burned-out corporate Netrunner looking for a way back in.”

steps.title: Name & handle
steps.body:
Enter your real {{ref:cs-name}} and the street {{ref:cs-handle}} people actually call you by.
Example: Name “Eliza Cortez”, Handle “Static”.

steps.title: Role & age
steps.body:
Pick your {{ref:cs-role}} — this auto-fills your Special Ability — and set your {{ref:cs-age}}.
Example: Role Netrunner (Special Ability: Interface), Age 27.

steps.title: Lifepath — origins & style
steps.body:
In {{sec:lifepath}}, set cultural origin, personality and clothing/affectation. Use the roll buttons or type your own.
Example: Anglo-American, cynical personality, generic chic clothing, mirrorshades.

steps.title: Lifepath — family
steps.body:
Fill in family background, parents and siblings in {{sec:lifepath}}.
Example: corporate family that lost everything; one estranged older brother.

steps.title: Lifepath — motivations
steps.body:
Note what you value most, how you feel about people, and your most valued person and possession.
Example: values honor; wary of strangers; most valued person is a dead mentor; prized possession is their first deck.

steps.title: Life events
steps.body:
Roll year-by-year events with “Roll by age” to build your history, or add them manually.
Example: age 22 — made a powerful enemy; age 25 — big score that went sideways.

steps.title: Stats
steps.body:
Assign your nine stats in {{sec:stats}} using your GM's method (CP2020 points or 9D10). Derived values update automatically.
Example (Fast/Friendly, ~62 pts): INT 8, REF 7, TECH 8, COOL 6, ATT 5, LUCK 6, MA 6, BODY 6, EMP 7.

steps.title: Skills
steps.body:
Spend your starting points ({{ref:skill-points}}, 40 by default) in {{sec:skills}}. Put points into the highlighted career skills first.
Example: Interface +6, Programming +5, Education +4, then round out with the rest.

steps.title: Special Ability
steps.body:
Confirm your role's Special Ability rank at the top of {{sec:skills}} — it was set when you chose your role.
Example: a Netrunner's Interface should usually be one of your highest skills.

steps.title: Cyberware & humanity
steps.body:
Install chrome in {{sec:cyber}} and keep an eye on the Humanity Cost {{ref:cyber-hc-total}}. High cost pushes you toward cyberpsychosis.
Example: Interface Plugs (2d6 HC) and a Chipware Socket.

steps.title: Netrunner gear
steps.body:
You're a Netrunner — set up your deck, programs and interface in {{sec:net}}.
Example: a Mensa-grade deck loaded with Hammer, Worm and a Shield program.

steps.title: Weapons
steps.body:
Pick your weapons in {{sec:weapons}}.
Example: a Militech Avenger (medium pistol) and a knife as backup.

steps.title: Fashion & armor
steps.body:
Equip an outfit in {{sec:fashion}}. The equipped outfit's armor SP feeds your damage track.
Example: an armored leather jacket (SP 14) over generic chic.

steps.title: Inventory & vehicles
steps.body:
Stock your gear in {{sec:inventory}} and add any {{sec:vehicles}}.
Example: medkit, flashlight, agent (pocket computer); a beat-up city car.

steps.title: Lifestyle & money
steps.body:
Set up {{sub:lifestyle:Housing}}, {{sub:lifestyle:Services}}, banking and cash in {{sec:lifestyle}}.
Example: Combat Zone cube (200 eb/mo), 1,000 eb cash, no debts.

steps.title: Network, health & notes
steps.body:
Add contacts and gigs in {{sec:network}}, check your health track in {{sec:life}}, and jot anything in {{sec:notes}}.
Example: Fixer “Dex” as a contact; note your eddies and any debts in Notes.
